name: No witnesses to my chemical exposure at work in Bountiful, does that kill my claim?
description: No, use medical proof and records—presumption aids on-site exposures.[1]

mainEntity.name: No witnesses to my chemical exposure at work in Bountiful, does that kill my claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, use medical proof and records—presumption aids on-site exposures.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: No, medical evidence and records can suffice; Utah presumes on-premises claims work-related.[1] Witnesses help but not required.[1] Key factors: Exposure logs, co-worker affidavits, testing; doctor causation report.[1] Reconstruct timeline with emails/reports. Legal guidance strengthens silent-exposure cases.
